臧.origin: Made with 臣 (official); it is an old word meaning good or proper, and a surname.

sentence.en: Zang is a surname.

自.origin: A picture of a nose; in China people point to their nose to mean self.

sentence.en: I'll do it myself.

臭.origin: The nose 自 above a dog 犬 sniffing; it means stinky.

sentence.en: The trash smells bad.

至.origin: Pictures a bird coming down to the ground 土; it means to reach or arrive.

sentence.en: I still remember it to this day.

致.origin: Reaching 至 with action 攵; it means to send or to cause.

sentence.en: Everyone is in agreement.

臼.origin: A picture of a mortar used to grind grain; it means mortar.

sentence.en: A stone mortar can pound rice.

舀.origin: A hand 爫 reaching into a container 臼; it means to scoop or ladle.

sentence.en: He ladled out a bowl of soup.

舂.origin: Hands pounding inside a mortar 臼; it means to pound grain.

sentence.en: Grandma is pounding rice.

舅.origin: Made with 男 (man); it means mother's brother, an uncle.

sentence.en: My uncle came to my house.

舌.origin: A tongue sticking out of a mouth 口; it means tongue.

sentence.en: He stuck out his tongue.

舒.origin: Made with 舍 (house); it means relaxed and comfortable.

sentence.en: This bed is very comfortable.

舔.origin: The tongue radical 舌; it means to lick.

sentence.en: The puppy licks my hand.

般.origin: A pole 殳 turning a boat 舟; it came to mean kind, sort, or manner.

sentence.en: He usually gets up at eight.

舰.origin: The boat radical 舟; it means a warship.

sentence.en: There is a warship at sea.

舱.origin: A storeroom 仓 on a boat 舟; it means a cabin or ship's hold.

sentence.en: It is warm inside the cabin.

舵.origin: The boat radical 舟; it means a rudder that steers a ship.

sentence.en: He is steering the boat.

舷.origin: The boat radical 舟; it means the side of a ship.

sentence.en: He stands at the side of the ship.

艇.origin: The boat radical 舟; it means a small light boat.

sentence.en: There is a small boat on the lake.

良.origin: An old character that means good, fine, or kind.

sentence.en: She is very kind.

色.origin: Shows a person ⺈ and 巴; it means color.

sentence.en: I like the color red.

艳.origin: Lush 丰 and full of color 色; it means bright and beautiful.

sentence.en: The flowers are bright and colorful.

艾.origin: The grass radical 艹; it is the name of a plant, mugwort.

sentence.en: Mugwort hangs on the door.

芦.origin: The grass radical 艹; it means reed.

sentence.en: Reeds grow all along the river.

芯.origin: The heart 心 of a plant 艹; it means the core or center.

sentence.en: The pencil lead broke.

苏.origin: The grass radical 艹; it means to revive or wake up, and is a place name.

sentence.en: He slowly came to.

苑.origin: The grass radical 艹; it means a garden or park.

sentence.en: There are many flowers in the garden.

苞.origin: A flower 艹 wrapped up 包; it means a flower bud.

sentence.en: There are flower buds on the tree.

若.origin: An old character meaning if, or like.

sentence.en: If it rains, we go home.

苫.origin: The grass radical 艹; it means a straw mat used to cover things.

sentence.en: Cover the grain with a straw mat.

范.origin: The grass radical 艹; it means a model or example, and is a surname.

sentence.en: This is a writing model.

茅.origin: The grass radical 艹; it means a kind of tall grass, thatch.

sentence.en: The roof is covered with thatch.

茎.origin: The grass radical 艹; it means the stem of a plant.

sentence.en: The flower stem is thin and long.

茧.origin: A silk shell 艹 spun by a worm 虫; it means cocoon.

sentence.en: The silkworm has spun a cocoon.

茬.origin: The grass radical 艹; it means a crop stubble, or a turn of something.

sentence.en: Wheat stubble is left in the field.

茹.origin: The grass radical 艹; an old word meaning to eat vegetables.

sentence.en: Grandma eats vegetarian all year.

荆.origin: A thorny plant 艹 with blades 刂; it means brambles or thorns.

sentence.en: The mountain path is full of thorns.

荒.origin: The grass radical 艹; it means wild, barren, or wasteland.

sentence.en: This is a stretch of wasteland.

荚.origin: The grass radical 艹; it means a seed pod.

sentence.en: There are beans inside the pod.

荡.origin: Water 汤 sweeping over plants 艹; it means to sway or to rinse.

sentence.en: The little boat sways on the water.

荤.origin: The grass radical 艹; it means meat dishes, the opposite of vegetarian.

sentence.en: There is a meat dish on the table.

荷.origin: The grass radical 艹; it means lotus.

sentence.en: The pond is full of lotus blossoms.

荻.origin: The grass radical 艹; it means a kind of reed grass.

sentence.en: Reed grass grows by the river.

莫.origin: An old character meaning do not, or none.

sentence.en: Please do not speak loudly.

莲.origin: The grass radical 艹; it means lotus.

sentence.en: The lotus flower is so pretty.

获.origin: An old character meaning to catch or to gain.

sentence.en: There was a big harvest this year.

菊.origin: The grass radical 艹; it means chrysanthemum.

sentence.en: Chrysanthemums bloom in autumn.

菌.origin: The grass radical 艹; it means fungus, mushroom, or germ.

sentence.en: Washing hands removes germs.

菱.origin: The grass radical 艹; it means water caltrop, a pond plant.

sentence.en: Water caltrops grow in the water.

萍.origin: The grass radical 艹 with water; it means duckweed floating on ponds.

sentence.en: Duckweed floats on the water.

萤.origin: An insect 虫 that glows; it means firefly.

sentence.en: There are fireflies on summer nights.
